args.title: make programming fun again — rerun under 0.11.0
args.subject: q:how must the agentic-coding process change so the human keeps skill and the software resists aging

args.text: A discussion's charter is exactly what accountable deciding runs on: the presupposition is not smuggled in, it is the recorded subject of this discussion. Contesting it is a legal move — reframe THIS discussion — and that move stays available on the record; a charter you can reframe is not an unaccountable document.
args.author: reframe-1

args.text: Per-team relativism dissolves the question rather than answering it: aging costs land on future maintainers who are not in the room, so a rule letting the present team discount them restates the principal-agent problem as its own solution.
args.author: maint-1

args.text: Scope-policing is circular: citing the prompt's presuppositions to exclude velocity-first assumes the prompt-writer's values are the governing ones — the question was WHO decides, and 'the document does' is an answer nobody can be held accountable to.
args.author: ship-1

args.text: The adopting team decides: velocity-first is the revealed preference of most shops, and a process that ignores that is shelfware. Governance is per-team — each prices its own comprehension insurance against its own incident tolerance.
args.author: ship-1

args.text: Scope grounds: the problem statement presupposes mastery and longevity matter — it names skill decay and aging as harms. Velocity therefore constrains the HOW, never the WHETHER; the mastery-first ranking governs this discussion, and a velocity-first shop is answering a different question than the one asked.
args.author: reframe-1

args.text: Whose value ranking governs the agentic-coding process — is the velocity dividend spendable on comprehension, and who decides?
args.author: reframe-1

args.text: The testing effect is among the most replicated results in learning science, and code-comprehension studies show active recall of system behavior outperforms re-reading — the premise holds where it is used.
args.author: learn-1

args.text: Hot-spot mining and dependency centrality identify the load-bearing core empirically, and it shifts slowly relative to feature churn — the core is measurable, not mystical.
args.author: analog-1

args.text: The sum-of-costs premise assumes overlapping scopes the design explicitly avoids: contracts price only boundaries, holdership only the load-bearing core, sampling only merges into it, and the periphery pays nothing. Total overhead is bounded by the size of the core — which the partition keeps a minority — so the bundle's cost is the max of its slices, not the sum of its parents.
args.author: maint-1

args.text: The bundle stacks four process taxes — contract authorship, paired holdership, sampled interrogation, scheduled handoffs. Each is defensible alone; jointly they consume the velocity dividend that justified agents in the first place. A synthesis whose cost is the sum of its parents' costs has answered the objections of none of them.
args.author: ship-1

args.text: Risk-priced comprehension: (1) the human authors the contract layer — module boundaries, invariants, property tests — which stays human-sized; (2) load-bearing modules carry paired theory-holdership with scheduled handoff implementations (per the sijol-pizup decision); (3) merge interrogation is sampled by criticality and churn and feeds spaced retrieval — never blanket-gated; (4) everywhere else, generation runs at full speed and comprehension is bought on demand — the do-nothing policy applied exactly where its assumption holds.
args.author: analog-1

args.text: The arithmetic closes because holdership is a role attached to work already flowing, not new headcount: handoff implementations are real backlog items, and the load-bearing core is by definition a minority of the module graph — the marginal cost is pairing overhead on a few PRs a week, which is measurable and cappable.
args.author: mastery-1

args.text: Two current holders per load-bearing module is headcount arithmetic that doesn't close: a ten-module core demands twenty holder-seats plus rotation overhead, from a team agentic coding was supposed to make leaner — the invariant is priced in the scarcest resource and nobody has budgeted it.
args.author: ship-1

args.text: Paired theory-holdership: every load-bearing module has two CURRENT theory-holders; holdership rotates one seat at a time through scheduled handoff implementations — the incoming holder hand-implements a real change under the outgoing holder's review. Individual depth is the mechanism; team-level coverage (N=2, rotating) is the invariant.
args.author: reframe-1

args.text: A single current theory-holder IS the bus-factor this question was raised from: vacation, attrition, or reassignment orphans the module's theory overnight — a guarantee of one is not a guarantee.
args.author: learn-1

args.text: Distributed comprehension at agent scale is a mirage: theory transfers between heads slower than agents produce code, so guaranteeing that ANY member can predict guarantees it only shallowly — the invariant is unenforceable precisely where it matters.
args.author: firstp-1

args.text: The individual is the unit: comprehension is a property of a head, not an org chart — teams comprehend only through individuals who do. The process must produce at least one CURRENT theory-holder per load-bearing module, and rotate holdership deliberately.
args.author: mastery-1

args.text: The team is the unit: the process must guarantee distributed comprehension — any competent member can predict behavior in the load-bearing modules. Individual mastery is a means, not the goal; a process that produces one expert has failed.
args.author: learn-1

args.text: Whose comprehension is the unit that matters — the individual programmer's or the team's — and what does the answer demand of an agentic process?
args.author: reframe-1

args.text: Airlines don't hand-fly random legs: recency requirements target specific maneuvers within currency windows. The analogy imports targeting with it — draw the mandated item from this week's highest-churn load-bearing module and the flat tax becomes insurance priced by risk.
args.author: analog-1

args.text: This defeats blanket gating, not interrogation: sample-based interrogation calibrated to module criticality keeps the retrieval effect — spaced retrieval works better with gaps — while removing the per-diff bottleneck. The objection's aviation lesson (target the high-consequence regime) is an amendment, not a refutation.
args.author: learn-1

args.text: The position requires human AUTHORSHIP of the contract layer, not approval of generated contracts — authorship is the mechanism. Unlike code, the contract layer is small enough for authorship to be enforced and audited (spec churn and review latency expose rubber-stamping).
args.author: maint-1

args.text: Bus-factor argues for rotating core ownership, not against the partition: the position's load-bearing claim is that a core small enough for one head exists at all — rotation and pairing then distribute it. The alternative, everyone owning everything, is how nobody owns anything.
args.author: mastery-1

args.text: This proves too much: it implies every merge requires full theory, a bar no team has met since code review went async — systems survive on partial theory plus regression nets. The live question is the marginal value of pre-merge comprehension per diff, which for most agent diffs is low.
args.author: ship-1

args.text: Naur: a program's theory lives in people and cannot be reconstructed from artifacts alone; rebuilding it mid-incident is when reconstruction is slowest and least reliable — the assumption inverts the empirical order of theory and artifact.
args.author: firstp-1

args.text: Blanket prediction-gates tax every diff equally; at agent-scale merge volume the human becomes the bottleneck or the questions degrade into perfunctory trivia — aviation's lesson is that spot-checks work when targeted at rare, high-consequence regimes, not applied to every landing.
args.author: analog-1

args.text: Hand-writing the core preserves the author's skill but walls it into one head: the partition concentrates the program's theory in whoever owns the core while everyone else decays exactly as before — it converts a skill problem into a bus-factor problem.
args.author: learn-1

args.text: Specs, invariants, and property tests are exactly the artifact class agents now write best; a human approving generated contracts retains no more theory than one approving generated code — the boundary layer quietly becomes agent-owned too, and the human is again a spectator.
args.author: mastery-1

args.text: A weekly hand-written work item is a fixed tax paid in the slowest currency: it neither targets the modules where misunderstanding is riskiest nor scales as the codebase grows — a ritual that feels virtuous while covering an ever-smaller fraction of the system.
args.author: ship-1

args.text: On-demand comprehension arrives after the damage: aging is caused by changes made without the program's theory, and incidents are its lagging indicator — by the time a deep-dive is forced, the structure is already compromised and the dive rediscovers less than was lost.
args.author: maint-1

args.text: Comprehension can be rebuilt on demand fast enough to be useful mid-incident
args.author: firstp-1

args.text: Skill decay is driven by loss of retrieval practice, not by tool use per se — the testing effect transfers to code comprehension
args.author: firstp-1

args.text: There exists a stable, identifiable load-bearing core: criticality is knowable in advance rather than emergent from use
args.author: firstp-1

args.text: Aviation automation policy: as airlines mandate hand-flown legs to counter automation dependency, mandate a scheduled hand-implemented work item each week inside code the agent normally owns, with the agent forbidden from touching that diff.
args.author: analog-1

args.text: Invert the review: before merge, the agent interrogates the human — prediction questions about the diff's behavior, failure modes, and layout; unanswered questions block the merge and feed a spaced-repetition queue. Reading rots; retrieval sticks.
args.author: learn-1

args.text: Architecture as executable contract: the human authors module boundaries, invariants, and property tests as machine-checked specs; agents may only fill in implementations that pass them. Aging is fought at the boundary layer, which stays human-sized.
args.author: maint-1

args.text: Partition by criticality: the human hand-writes and owns the load-bearing core — the modules whose failure or misdesign is expensive — and agents generate the periphery against the core's interfaces. Skill is spent where it compounds.
args.author: mastery-1

args.text: Change nothing structural: keep full-speed generation and buy comprehension on demand — when an incident or a gnarly change requires it, the human does a focused deep-dive with the agent as guide. Comprehension is purchased exactly when it pays.
args.author: ship-1

args.text: Given that agentic coding is here to stay, how must the day-to-day process of agentic coding change so that the human programmer retains skill and domain knowledge, and the software resists Parnas-style aging?
args.author: reframe-1

args.basis: subsumption: handoff implementations are the hand-flown legs, risk-targeted per the objection
args.author: analog-1

args.basis: subsumption: sampled interrogation keeps retrieval, drops blanket gating per the objection
args.author: learn-1

args.basis: subsumption: the human-authored contract layer is clause 1 verbatim
args.author: maint-1

args.basis: subsumption: partition-by-criticality is the hybrid's scoping mechanism
args.author: mastery-1

args.basis: subsumption: clause 4 is the do-nothing policy scoped to where its assumption survived
args.author: ship-1

args.basis: charter grounds; the circularity objection was answered on the record — reframing the discussion remains the legal recourse
unique justified position; survived the sum-of-costs stress-test; velocity-first sensitivity recorded and governed by the vufuf-kotar decision
coverage and depth both satisfied; the cost objection was answered on the record
